ResearchGate page did not return article body text, likely behind authentication wall."}, {"source_url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12348657/", "source_title": "Comprehensive Evaluation and Analysis of Aging Performance of Polymer-Rich Anchoring Adhesives", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.58, "year": 2025, "authors": [], "venue": "Polymers", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Hygrothermal (damp heat) aging treatment at 50 C and 98% humidity caused bending strength reductions of 16-51% in polymer-rich anchoring adhesives depending on adhesive composition, with sampling periods up to 3000 hours.", "direct_quote": "hygrothermal and water-curing treatments caused reductions of 16-51% and 15-77%, respectively, depending on adhesive composition. For the heat-moisture treatment test, the temperature was 50 degrees C, and the humidity was 98%. The sampling periods were 500 h, 1000 h, 1500 h, and 3000 h", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.72, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["hygrothermal aging", "bending strength", "50 C", "98% humidity", "3000 hours"]}, {"statement": "Alkali treatment was the most severe aging condition, reducing bending strength by up to 70% for sample 5# (modified epoxy resin) within 500 hours before stabilizing.", "direct_quote": "Alkali treatment reduced bending strength by up to 70% (sample 5#) within 500 h before stabilizing, while hygrothermal and water-curing treatments caused reductions of 16-51% and 15-77%, respectively, depending on adhesive composition", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.58, "confidence": 0.91, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["alkali treatment", "bending strength", "70%", "500 hours", "modified epoxy resin"]}, {"statement": "Dynamic thermomechanical analysis revealed significant loss factor decreases in epoxy adhesives after 500 hours of aging, with values dropping from above 1.0 to stable lower values, indicating increased material rigidity.", "direct_quote": "Dynamic thermomechanical analysis revealed significant loss factor decreases (e.g., epoxy adhesives dropped from >1.0 to stable lower values after 500 h aging), indicating increased rigidity. Dynamic mechanical analysis was conducted in three-point bending mode under a frequency of 1 Hz, with temperature ramping from ambient to 200 degrees C", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.55, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["DMA", "loss factor", "epoxy adhesives", "500 hours", "rigidity"]}, {"statement": "Infrared spectroscopy confirmed chemical degradation under aging including ester group breakage in vinyl ester resins with peak shifts at 1700 cm-1 and 1100 cm-1 and molecular chain scission in unsaturated polyesters.", "direct_quote": "Infrared spectroscopy confirmed chemical degradation, including ester group breakage in vinyl ester resins (peak shifts at 1700 cm-1 and 1100 cm-1) and molecular chain scission in unsaturated polyesters. The three test methods consistently demonstrated that 500 h of aging sufficiently captured performance trends", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.52, "confidence": 0.89, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["FTIR", "ester group breakage", "vinyl ester resin", "1700 cm-1", "1100 cm-1"]}, {"statement": "Six types of polymer-rich anchoring adhesives were tested per GB/T-2567-2021 standard including three modified epoxy resin, two modified vinyl ester resin, and one non-vinyl ester unsaturated polyester resin adhesive.", "direct_quote": "Six kinds of polymer-rich anchoring adhesives filled with fillers were selected, including three kinds of modified epoxy resin adhesives, two kinds of modified vinyl ester resin adhesives, and one kind of non-vinyl ester unsaturated polyester resin adhesive. The adhesive bulk specimens were prepared according to the requirements of GB/T-2567-2021", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.48, "confidence": 0.92, "perspective": "Regulatory", "entities": ["GB/T-2567-2021", "epoxy resin", "vinyl ester resin", "unsaturated polyester"]}], "evidence_summary": "Comprehensive aging study of polymer-rich anchoring adhesives under hygrothermal (50C/98%RH), alkali, and water bath conditions. Hygrothermal aging caused 16-51% bending strength loss. DMA showed increased rigidity; FTIR confirmed ester group breakage."}]}
